notes: Each eval drives the axon4to5-migrate Skill with the query-gateway recipe in single mode. Coverage: REST controller import-only (Class<R> overload already used), ResponseType wrapper removal + queryMany, sync callback bare-.get()-to-orTimeout, rejected handler class with QueryGateway field.

evals.name: spring-rest-import-only
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `1: spring-rest-import-only` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/40-query-handlers/query-handler.md`. Reach for `examples/query-handlers/06-caller-rest-controller-import-only.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** DwellingRestController is a Spring @RestController. It imports AF4 QueryGateway (org.axonframework.queryhandling.QueryGateway) and calls `queryGateway.query(new GetDwellingById(dwellingId), DwellingReadModel.class)` — already using the Class<R> overload. Apply the query-gateway recipe: (1) swap the QueryGateway import to AF5 (org.axonframework.messaging.queryhandling.gateway.QueryGateway). Body, field, constructor, return type, and method signature stay identical.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-query-gateway` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: AF5 QueryGateway import; body unchanged; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-query-gateway

evals.name: spring-rest-response-type-removal
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `2: spring-rest-response-type-removal` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/40-query-handlers/query-handler.md`, `patterns/40-query-handlers/query-response-types.md`. Reach for `examples/query-handlers/08-caller-response-type-wrapper-removal.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** BikeStatusController is a Spring @RestController. It uses `ResponseTypes.instanceOf(BikeStatus.class)` for a single-response query and `ResponseTypes.multipleInstancesOf(BikeStatus.class)` for a multi-response query. Apply the matching patterns: (1) swap QueryGateway import to AF5, (2) replace ResponseTypes.instanceOf(BikeStatus.class) with BikeStatus.class, (3) replace query(..., ResponseTypes.multipleInstancesOf(BikeStatus.class)) with queryMany(..., BikeStatus.class), (4) remove the ResponseTypes import.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-query-gateway` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: AF5 QueryGateway import; ResponseTypes wrappers removed; queryMany used for multi-response; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-query-gateway

evals.name: blocking-get-to-async-upgrade
evals.prompt:
You are running eval `3: blocking-get-to-async-upgrade` for the `axon4to5-migrate` Skill. The Skill lives at `$SKILL_DIR`. Load `$SKILL_DIR/SKILL.md`, `$SKILL_DIR/patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`, and (if you need fuller before/after walkthroughs) `$SKILL_DIR/examples/ALL_EXAMPLES.md`.

**Skip SKILL.md Step 1** (the Q1/Q2/Q3 AskUserQuestion prompts) — pin these answers directly: `configuration=spring`, Migration approach **B (AI only)**, `skip-openrewrite=true` (the harness has no Maven/Gradle project and you cannot invoke another Skill recursively). **Skip Step 2.5** progress tracking — there is no project root, just a single workspace directory.

The migration target is the file at `$SOURCE`. Its parent directory `$WORKSPACE/outputs/` is the working directory; treat any `secondary_fixtures` siblings already staged there as additional in-scope files. Migrate every target file IN PLACE.

**Identify the AF4 shapes in `$SOURCE` against `patterns/ALL_IN_ONE.md`** and apply the matching pattern(s). The patterns this eval exercises are: `patterns/40-query-handlers/query-handler.md`. Reach for `examples/query-handlers/07-caller-blocking-get-to-async.md` when you need a full before/after walkthrough.

**Eval-specific guidance:** DwellingsQueryService is a @Component with method `getDwelling(String dwellingId)` that returns `DwellingReadModel` and calls `queryGateway.query(new GetDwellingById(dwellingId), DwellingReadModel.class).get()` (bare blocking call). The method is a plain service method with no sync contract constraint. Apply the matching patterns: Path A (preferred): (1) swap QueryGateway import to AF5, (2) change method return type from `DwellingReadModel` to `CompletableFuture<DwellingReadModel>`, (3) simplify the body to `return queryGateway.query(new GetDwellingById(dwellingId), DwellingReadModel.class)` (no .get(), no try-catch), (4) add `import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture`.

Write the `**Result:**` block (per SKILL.md Step 4d) to `$WORKSPACE/outputs/result.md`. Required format: `**Result:** ✅ Success`, followed by `**Recipe:** axon4to5-query-gateway` and a `**Notes:**` line summarising what was changed.
evals.expected_output: AF5 QueryGateway import; method return type upgraded to CompletableFuture<DwellingReadModel>; body returns future directly; no .get(); CompletableFuture import added; Result: ✅ Success.
expectations: Result block marks Success with axon4to5-query-gateway

assertions.text: Method return type upgraded to CompletableFuture<DwellingReadModel>
